reactant_pattern_text: alcohol, alkene, epoxide, or halide capable of generating a carbocation
product_pattern_text: rearranged carbocation-derived alkene or substitution product
common_conditions: protic acid, Lewis acid, or ionization conditions that generate carbocations
description_short: Carbocation rearrangement involving 1,2-alkyl, hydride, or aryl shifts to reorganize molecular skeletons under acidic or ionizing conditions.

transformation_type: controlled acyl substitution to ketones or aldehydes
mechanism_type: organometallic addition to N-methoxy-N-methylamide with chelation control
reactant_pattern_text: acid chloride / anhydride / ester / lactone -> Weinreb amide + organometallic reagent
common_conditions: formation of Weinreb amide followed by low-temperature organometallic addition and acidic workup
synonym_names: Weinreb reaction|Weinreb ketone synthesis|Weinreb amide ketone synthesis
description_short: Preparation and use of N-methoxy-N-methylamides to convert carboxylic acid derivatives into ketones or aldehydes with single-addition control.

mechanism_type: concerted anti fragmentation related to Grob-type cleavage
reactant_pattern_text: cyclic or acyclic 1,3-diol monosulfonate or related substrate
product_pattern_text: medium-sized cyclic alkene or unsaturated fragment plus leaving-group fragment
common_conditions: strong base-promoted fragmentation of sulfonate derivatives
description_short: Base-induced stereospecific fragmentation of 1,3-diol monotosylates or related substrates to generate unsaturated fragments, often medium-ring alkenes.

transformation_type: conversion of epoxyketones to allylic alcohols
mechanism_type: hydrazone formation, epoxide opening, and nitrogen extrusion / transposition
common_conditions: epoxidation of enone followed by hydrazine treatment under acidic/basic conditions
description_short: Transformation of α,β-epoxyketones into allylic alcohols through hydrazine-mediated transposition with extrusion of nitrogen.

transformation_type: SN2 ether formation from alkoxides and alkyl electrophiles
mechanism_type: alkoxide substitution on alkyl halides or sulfonates
reactant_pattern_text: alkoxide or phenoxide + alkyl halide / sulfonate
product_pattern_text: dialkyl ether or aryl alkyl ether
common_conditions: base-generated alkoxide with alkyl halide or sulfonate, often heated or under phase-transfer conditions
description_short: Formation of ethers by SN2 reaction of alkoxides or phenoxides with alkyl electrophiles, including intra- and intermolecular variants.
